title: Security Event Log Generation Validation for Windows BES Hosts

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-007-6 R4 Part 4.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-007-6 R4 Part 4.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.33: Security Event Log Generation Validation for Windows BES Hosts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.33' running on index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, _internal forwarder metrics, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-48h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats latest(_time) as last_sec by host
| eval gap_h=round((now()-last_sec)/3600,2)
| where gap_h>24
| table host, bes_system, last_sec, gap_h
description: We detect Windows systems where the Security channel stopped forwarding while the host remains online, risking missing auditable events.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, WinEventLog:Security`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tune gap threshold per risk. (2) Exclude maintenance windows. (3) Alert server owners and Splunk admins. (4) Track ticket closure with log resume proof. Indexes required: `index=wineventlog`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security``; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=wineventlog` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `_internal` forwarder metrics.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tune gap threshold per risk. (2) Exclude maintenance windows. (3) Alert server owners and Splunk admins. (4) Track ticket closure with log resume proof.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-48h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats latest(_time) as last_sec by host
| eval gap_h=round((now()-last_sec)/3600,2)
| where gap_h>24
| table host, bes_system, last_sec, gap_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Event Log Generation Validation for Windows BES Hosts (CIP-007-6 R4 Part 4.2)** — We detect Windows systems where the Security channel stopped forwarding while the host remains online, risking missing auditable events.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=wineventlog` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `_internal` forwarder metrics. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wineventlog; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wineventlog, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap_h>24` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bes_system, gap_h, host, last_sec.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale log sources), Single value (host count), Timeline
